title: Creating A Moore’s Law For AI Scaling
gist: imec and partners argue AI scaling now requires a 10X compute-efficiency gain over the next decade achievable only via multiplicative full-stack co-optimization, because data center power scales linearly and multi-agentic/physical AI will demand up to 150X more compute. The piece details concrete roadmap pivots: CFETs at the 7A node (~2033), CMOS 2.0, IGZO/ferroelectric 3D DRAM, hybrid bonding for taller HBM stacks, and imec's 'volumetric 3D' active interposer.

themes.theme: The AI scaling crisis and 10X efficiency mandate

insights.claim: Processing 10X the number of AI algorithms currently requires roughly 10X the amount of data center hardware, because power consumption rises almost linearly and does not scale.
insights.speaker: Laura Peters
insights.so_what: Linear power scaling is the structural ceiling on AI economics; the winners will be whoever breaks the compute-per-watt curve, not whoever builds the biggest data center.
insights.quote: power consumption increases almost linearly. It does not really scale.
insights.locator: AI data centers are now literally measured in gigawatts

insights.claim: As workloads shift from training to inference with multi-agentic AI systems, researchers estimate these systems will require 150X the compute power of existing large language models.
insights.speaker: Laura Peters
insights.so_what: A 150X inference compute jump implies sustained demand for accelerators, HBM, and power infrastructure well beyond current training buildouts.
insights.quote: these systems will require 150X the compute power of existing large language models
insights.locator: As workloads shift from training to inferencing

insights.claim: Meeting AI demand requires a 10X gain in compute efficiency over just 10 years, achievable only because algorithms, device scaling, communication fabric, and architecture are multiplicative contributors that exceed AI's needs when co-optimized.
insights.speaker: Patrick Vandenameele
insights.so_what: Reframes the roadmap as a coordination problem: value accrues to ecosystem orchestrators (imec model) rather than any single layer.
insights.quote: the main contributors, algorithms, device scaling, communication fabric, and architectural advances are multiplicative

insights.claim: Mobile application processors are migrating to 2nm technology by the end of 2026, RF devices to 6nm finFET, and camera image signal processors to 12nm finFET, driven by AI at the edge.
insights.speaker: Kevin Zhang
insights.so_what: AI is pulling consumer and automotive silicon to leading-edge nodes faster, sustaining TSMC's advanced-node demand beyond pure HPC.
insights.quote: application processors are migrating to 2nm technology by the end of this year
insights.locator: He noted that in mobile phones

insights.claim: Driving operating voltage down to 0.4V achieves up to 70% switching power reduction in edge devices.
insights.speaker: Kevin Zhang
insights.so_what: Ultra-low-voltage operation is the lever for battery-constrained edge AI, a key differentiator for mobile SoC competitiveness.
insights.quote: We are driving operating voltage down to 0.4V in order to achieve up to 70% switching power reduction.
insights.locator: And nothing is more important than operating voltage

themes.theme: Physical AI demands heterogeneous adaptive architectures

insights.claim: Physical AI is a time-bounded response problem where the control loop must finish within microseconds every cycle, unlike latency-tolerant cloud inference where mistakes can simply be retried.
insights.speaker: Salil Raje
insights.so_what: Microsecond determinism favors FPGAs and adaptive SoCs over GPU-centric architectures in robotics and autonomous systems, an opening for AMD's adaptive computing group.
insights.quote: the control group has to finish within microseconds, every cycle, continuously
insights.locator: Raje noted that rapid response is essential

insights.claim: The top 150 robotics OEMs and developers want determinism, predictable timing, open architecture, and a scalable platform that works across industrial arms, AMRs, and humanoid robots.
insights.speaker: Salil Raje
insights.so_what: Demand for one reusable platform across robot form factors signals consolidation toward whichever vendor delivers open, deterministic adaptive silicon.
insights.quote: The top 150 robotics OEMs and developers have three asks — determinism, predictable timing, open architecture, and scalable platform.
insights.locator: The key insight is that physical AI systems

insights.claim: imec projects the first production use of complementary FETs (vertically stacked nFETs and pFETs) at the 7 angstrom node around 2033, requiring both front- and backside metallization.
insights.speaker: Laura Peters
insights.so_what: CFETs at 2033 mark the post-gate-all-around scaling frontier; tool and materials suppliers should align development cycles to this timeline.
insights.locator: Imec’s latest roadmap projects the first production use

insights.claim: At the 7A node, CMOS 2.0 will combine a functional wafer backside with tier-based frontside functions dedicating layers to specific device types like memory, logic, and RF within SoCs.
insights.speaker: Laura Peters
insights.so_what: CMOS 2.0 rewrites SoC design rules toward functional partitioning, reshaping EDA and packaging requirements years out.
insights.locator: At the 7 Å node, CMOS 2.0 will combine

insights.claim: Switching DRAM to an IGZO oxide semiconductor vertical channel can reduce off-current by several orders of magnitude, and adding ferroelectric materials can reduce operating voltage.
insights.speaker: Jaihyuk Song
insights.so_what: IGZO and ferroelectric materials are concrete enablers for 3D DRAM power reduction, signaling Samsung's memory differentiation path.
insights.quote: Switching to an oxide semiconductor channel can reduce the off-current by several orders of magnitude.
insights.locator: One recent material breakthrough is the development of IGZO

insights.claim: In HBM, hybrid bonding will enable higher die-count stacking of 12H, 16H, and 20H while reducing thermal resistance, and shifting some core logic from the GPU to the HBM base die improves speed and power efficiency.
insights.speaker: Jaihyuk Song
insights.so_what: Logic migration into the HBM base die blurs memory/compute boundaries, a value-shift toward advanced memory makers and packaging suppliers.
insights.locator: In HBM, hybrid bonding will enable higher die count stacking

insights.claim: Rather than scaling substrates from 5.5X reticle size toward 9X, 15X, or 300mm wafers, imec proposes a volumetric 3D active interposer that wraps around HBM modules to avoid yield issues of massive passive substrates.
insights.speaker: Eric Beyne
insights.so_what: If yield-driven volumetric 3D displaces ever-larger interposers, it reshuffles the advanced packaging supply chain and cooling approach.
insights.locator: rather than continuing to ramp substrate sizes

insights.claim: The packaging concern is not the cost of the substrate but its yield and reliability, because each HBM placed on a big interposer exceeds $1,000 and a non-yielding interposer forces scrapping everything on it.
insights.speaker: Eric Beyne
insights.so_what: High per-HBM value makes interposer yield the dominant cost risk, justifying investment in testable, repairable active interposers.
insights.quote: It’s not about the cost of the substrate, but its yield and reliability, because if it’s not yielding you have to throw away everything
insights.locator: If you look at these big interposers

insights.claim: Using its newly installed high-NA (0.55) ASML scanner, imec demonstrated 16nm pitch lines and spaces (8nm lines) and features separated by 8.7nm tip-to-tip spacing.
insights.speaker: Laura Peters
insights.so_what: Concrete high-NA resolution milestones validate ASML's tool roadmap for sub-2nm patterning demand.
insights.locator: Imec recently installed its first high-NA

insights.claim: UMC is leveraging imec's iSiPP300 integrated silicon photonics process to accelerate its own 12-inch silicon photonics platform, with a 12-inch PDK expected available to general customers in 2027.
insights.speaker: Steven Hsu
insights.so_what: AI's demand for higher-bandwidth optical interconnects is pushing photonics to 12-inch manufacturing, a 2027 inflection for optical I/O supply.
insights.quote: We expect to make our 12-inch silicon photonics PDK available to general customers in 2027
insights.locator: Combined with our internal R&D efforts
